zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Ufford II於1279年6月11日出生在英國素福爾可瑟斯頓時，他的父親Robert de Ufford I45歲，母親Mary de Say45歲。他於大約1297年與Cecily de Valoignes在英國素福爾可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1316年9月9日在英國素福爾可伍德布里奇享年37歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可布特利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ufford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Ufford (Northamptonshire), Ufford (Suffolk), or Offord Cluny or Offord Darcy (Huntingdonshire). The Northamptonshire and Suffolk placenames derive from the Old English personal name Uffa + Old English worth ‘enclosure’. For the Huntingdonshire placename see Offord , with which the surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Offord
meanings: English (East Anglia and London): habitational name from Offord Cluny and Offord Darcy in Huntindgonshire. The placename derives from Old English upp ‘up, upper’ + ford ‘ford’.
English: alternatively, from a lost place called Offord near Pennyford in Wootton Wawen, Warwickshire. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Offa + Old English worth ‘enclosure’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Ufford II was born on 11 June 1279, in Thurston, Suffolk, England, his father, Robert de Ufford I, was 45 and his mother, Mary de Say, was 45. He married Cecily de Valoignes about 1297, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 9 September 1316, in Woodbridge, Suffolk, England, at the age of 37, and was buried in Butley, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Ufford II est né le 11 juin 1279, à Thurston, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Ufford I, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Mary de Say, avait 45 ans. Il a épousé Cecily de Valoignes environ 1297, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 9 septembre 1316, à Woodbridge, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterré à Butley, Suffolk, Angleterre.

Er hat um 1297 in Suffolk, England Cecily de Valoignes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 9. September 1316 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Woodbridge, Suffolk, England und wurde in Butley, Suffolk, England begraben.

Lui sposato Cecily de Valoignes circa 1297, nel Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Ufford II nasceu em 11 de junho de 1279, em Thurston, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Ufford I, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Mary de Say, tinha 45 anos. Ele casou-se com Cecily de Valoignes aproximadamente 1297, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 9 de setembro de 1316, em Woodbridge, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 37 anos, e foi sepultado em Butley, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.Parham, Plomesgate, Suffolk, England: парам вит хачестон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Ufford II родился 11 июня 1279 г. в местности турстон, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Ufford I было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary de Say, 45. Он женился на женщине по имени Cecily de Valoignes около 1297 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 9 сентября 1316 г. в местности вудбридж, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 37 лет и был похоронен в местности батли, суффолк, Англия.
